# HyperION v1.0.4.0 — Playback Stability, Workflow Fixes & Reliable Plug-in Scanning

HyperION 1.0.4.0 is a maintenance release focused on playback stability, Pattern Mixer reliability, Arrangement editing, VST3 discovery, protected assets, crash diagnostics, and AI safety reporting.

## Highlights

- Fixed Pattern Mixer channel On/Off, Mute, and Solo behavior.
- Improved playback timing and eliminated several stale or repeating Pattern-audio cases.
- Reduced clicks and pops during clip playback, seeking, looping, and plug-in changes.
- Added the ability to fit analyzed audio to the project key without changing its speed.
- Fixed multi-clip duplication placement.
- Improved Waves and WaveShell plug-in discovery.
- Fixed the plug-in scanner progress bar not reaching completion.
- Prevented duplicate Browser entries originating from old build locations.
- Added improved Windows crash dumps and diagnostics.
- Added functional AI-generated content reporting with offline retry support.

## Playback and Audio Engine

- Fixed stale Pattern render data continuing to play after Pattern edits.
- Fixed old or lower-pitched Pattern notes returning during playback.
- Corrected Pattern root-note and per-step pitch handling.
- Improved sample-accurate Pattern scheduling across audio blocks.
- Improved long Arrangement playback stability and rhythmic timing.
- Cleared outdated routing and plug-in delay data after seeks, loops, stops, and other transport discontinuities.
- Prevented audio from the previous playhead position leaking into the new position.
- Added short safety ramps at clip boundaries to reduce clicks and pops.
- Improved clip overlap and crossfade transitions.
- Smoothed audio transitions while inserting, removing, bypassing, or reconfiguring plug-ins.
- Reduced interface stalls and stale audio while editing Patterns during playback.

## Pattern Mixer

- Fixed channel On/Off controls so disabled channels no longer process or output audio.
- Fixed Solo behavior for Pattern channels and the Pattern master.
- Corrected interactions between On/Off, Mute, and Solo states.
- Disabled Pattern master channels now clear their output correctly.
- Pattern channel enabled states are saved and restored with projects.
- Pattern audio is refreshed correctly after changing pitch, velocity, pan, sample, Mute, Solo, or power state.
- Improved Pattern channel power-control hit areas and visual feedback.
- Improved Pattern Mixer fader visibility and theme-aware colouring.
- Pattern sample previews now respect the configured lane velocity.

## Pitch Analysis

- Added **Fit to Project Key** after analyzing an audio clip.
- HyperION now compares the detected source key with the current project key.
- The nearest musical semitone offset is calculated automatically.
- Pitch fitting changes the clip's pitch without changing its duration or playback speed.
- Repeating the operation no longer accumulates additional transposition.
- The available pitch range is expanded automatically when necessary.

## Arrangement and Editing

- Fixed multi-clip duplication being positioned after the first selected clip.
- Selected clips are now duplicated as one complete group.
- The duplicated group is placed after the latest-ending selected clip.
- Relative timing and gaps between selected clips are preserved.
- Improved audio and MIDI selection handling during duplication.
- Improved clip fade validation after editing and trimming.
- Improved synchronization of reversed and processed clip states.

## VST3 and Waves Plug-ins

- Fixed the plug-in scan progress bar remaining below 100% after scanning completed.
- Improved WaveShell detection and plug-in enumeration.
- Increased the scan timeout for WaveShell because it may expose many individual Waves plug-ins.
- Previous WaveShell entries incorrectly placed on the blacklist are released for scanning again.
- Temporary timeouts and missing dependencies are now treated as retryable conditions.
- Slow or temporarily busy plug-ins are no longer permanently blacklisted automatically.
- Improved plug-in scanner result and error descriptions.
- Improved scanner isolation when a third-party plug-in fails.
- Strengthened the Windows crash barrier used when opening native plug-in editors.
- Improved fallback to HyperION's generic editor when a native editor cannot open safely.
- Improved hosted plug-in editor sizing, preset access, footer information, and theme integration.

## Browser and Protected Assets

- Fixed sounds appearing multiple times because assets from old build directories were also scanned.
- Browser scanning now uses one canonical protected asset location.
- Improved Browser cache migration and duplicate cleanup.
- Preserved sample author suffixes so legitimately different samples are not merged incorrectly.
- Fixed protected assets occasionally being reported as damaged during verification.
- Improved recovery when asset mounting is interrupted by an application or system shutdown.
- Fixed simultaneous asset-mount workers conflicting while creating directories.
- Existing valid mounted assets are reused instead of being rebuilt.
- Unchanged protected assets retain their hashes and do not require unnecessary updater downloads.

## Crash Diagnostics

- Added Windows minidump generation for unhandled HyperION crashes.
- Crash reports now include the exception, faulting module, process, thread, application version, and build information.
- Crash logs include the path of the matching dump file.
- Improved handling of both unhandled and vectored Windows exceptions.
- Improved diagnostics for HyperION, HyperION Plugin Scanner, and HyperION AI Setup crashes.

## AI Content Reporting

- Added **Report Issue** to the AI Music Generator and Prompt Audio Studio.
- Users can report inappropriate or problematic AI-generated results for review.
- Reports are stored locally before delivery is attempted.
- Reports are queued automatically when the review service is unavailable.
- Queued reports are retried after a later application launch.
- Successful reports receive a reference ID.
- Repeated delivery attempts do not create duplicate reports.
- Generated audio, license keys, device names, and machine fingerprints are not uploaded.

## Distribution and Updating

- Updated HyperION, HyperION Plugin Scanner, and HyperION AI Setup to version **1.0.4.0**.
- Corrected Microsoft Store package identity and publisher presentation.
- Improved Demo, Standard, and Ultimate edition packaging.
- Preserved Microsoft Store entitlement verification for production builds.
- Updated application and utility icons.
- Regenerated the differential update manifest without modifying unchanged protected assets.
- Existing projects, presets, settings, licenses, AI credentials, and project media remain compatible.
